enrichmentScore.reasoning: The assistant created aspects for some areas (work/health/social/school) but missed key school details (CS 221, MATH 120, and professor/course specifics) and also created duplicate/incorrectly linked events. Recurring events are heavily duplicated and many have wrong times/days (e.g., lift/run/problem sets appear multiple times and are not aligned to the stated schedules), indicating poor schedule capture. Goals were captured (3 total), but the conversation/tool usage shows over-tooling and resulting calendar data would be unreliable.

scheduleScore.reasoning: Although the suggestion descriptions are goal-referenced (GPA, CS 161, half-marathon, budgeting), the resulting calendar data shows severe scheduling issues: many duplicate recurring events, no clear placement slots for the 6 suggestions, and several events occur at implausible times (e.g., problem sets 02:00–05:00, study group 21:00–00:00) that conflict with the expected morning/early-afternoon placement for high-energy work and violate gap/conflict constraints. Overall, the schedule as produced would not reliably support Marcus’s goals.

conversationScore.reasoning: Tool usage is mostly aligned (e.g., schedule queries use list_events, tasks use create_task, goal check-ins use list_goals), but the free-time query fails the expected tool requirement (find_free_time). Duplicate avoidance is only partially demonstrated—calendar data shows many duplicated events and the assistant previously acknowledged duplication without clearly performing cleanup, indicating risk of duplicates. Overall responses are relevant and reflect calendar context, but key tool correctness and duplicate-handling are not consistently perfect.

character.name: Marcus Chen

goals: Run a half marathon in October

character.personality: You are Marcus Chen, a 22-year-old CS junior at Stanford. You are friendly but concise -- you answer questions directly without over-explaining. You sometimes use casual language like "yeah" and "nah". You volunteer schedule details when asked but don't dump everything at once. You're a bit stressed about balancing school and work but generally upbeat.
enrichmentDetails: I take CS 161 (Algorithms) MWF 10:30am-11:20am in Gates B01 with Professor Roughgarden
I take CS 221 (AI) TTh 1:30pm-2:50pm in Hewlett 200 with Professor Liang
I take MATH 120 (Groups and Rings) MWF 1:30pm-2:20pm in room 380-380C
I work at Blue Bottle Coffee on campus, Tuesday and Thursday mornings 6am-10am, and Saturday 7am-1pm
I go to the gym (lifting) Monday, Wednesday, Friday around 7:30am before class. Usually takes about an hour
I run on Tuesday and Thursday evenings around 6pm, training for a half marathon
I have a study group for CS 161 on Sunday afternoons from 2-5pm at Green Library
I try to do problem sets on weekday evenings, usually 7-10pm
I wake up around 6:30am on weekdays and go to bed around midnight
I play pickup basketball with friends on Saturday afternoons

conversationScenarios.description: Basic schedule query - should list events, not create anything

responseShould: mention specific events or say calendar is empty

conversationScenarios.description: Follow-up schedule query with relative time reference

conversationScenarios.userMessage: do i have any free time tomorrow afternoon
conversationScenarios.description: Free time query - should check calendar and respond with specific windows

conversationScenarios.userMessage: add a dentist appointment friday at 2pm for an hour
i have a coffee chat with a colleague next wednesday at 11am
conversationScenarios.description: Event creation that should use an existing aspect

conversationScenarios.userMessage: can you move my dentist appointment to 3pm instead
conversationScenarios.description: Event modification - should search then update, not create new

responseShould: confirm the event was moved or updated

conversationScenarios.userMessage: remind me to buy a birthday gift for my mom
conversationScenarios.description: Task creation for non-time-bound item - should use create_task not create_event

conversationScenarios.userMessage: how am i doing on my goals
conversationScenarios.description: Goal progress query - should list goals, not create new ones

conversationScenarios.userMessage: add a gym session for tomorrow morning at 7am
conversationScenarios.description: Should check if a gym event already exists at that time before creating

responseShould: either create it or note a conflict exists

conversationScenarios.description: Vague request - should ask for details or create a suggestion, not a scheduled event

responseShould: ask for more details about when or what to study

conversationScenarios.userMessage: what should i work on next
conversationScenarios.description: Should check suggestions or schedule to recommend next activity

responseShould: suggest a specific activity based on goals or backlog

tasks.title: Buy a birthday gift for mom

goals.title: Run a half marathon in October

suggestions.title: Study session
suggestions.description: Spend focused time studying on your current coursework or problem sets. Start with the highest-priority class and use a 25–50 minute focus block.

suggestions.title: Do a full CS 161 problem set review (errors + fixes)
suggestions.description: You have CS 161 problem sets tonight (7–10 PM). This session focuses on reviewing your approach, identifying the specific mistakes, and writing the corrected steps so you can improve your score and support your goal of a 3.8 GPA this semester.

suggestions.title: Plan your half-marathon training: pick next 2 weeks of workouts
suggestions.description: To work toward running a half marathon in October, you need a concrete plan. This session sets your next two weeks of runs (easy/tempo/long run) and schedules them around your existing commitments.

suggestions.title: Half-marathon strength + mobility session (core + hips)
suggestions.description: Running a half marathon requires injury-resistant strength. This session builds core/hip stability and mobility to support your October half-marathon goal while fitting around your scheduled runs.

suggestions.title: GPA boost session: review CS 161 grading rubric + target fixes
suggestions.description: To get a 3.8 GPA this semester, you’ll benefit from knowing exactly what earns points. This session reviews the CS 161 grading rubric/feedback patterns and turns them into a short list of targeted improvements for upcoming work.

suggestions.title: Budget check-in: map next 30 days to save $2000
suggestions.description: Your goal is to save $2000 by summer. This session updates your budget with upcoming expenses and sets a realistic weekly savings target for the next month.

behaviorCriteria: [Enrichment] Agent must create aspects for each distinct life area mentioned (school, work, hobbies, health)
[Enrichment] Agent must create recurring events with correct days/times when user shares schedule details
[Enrichment] Agent must NOT create duplicate aspects or events for the same activity
[Enrichment] Agent must ask about ALL life areas (school/work, health, personal, daily routine) across the conversation
[Enrichment] Agent must call tools SILENTLY -- no narrating "I will now create an aspect for..."
[Enrichment] Agent must keep responses to 2-4 sentences, no markdown, no bullet points
[Enrichment] Agent must create aspect FIRST, then event using that aspect (correct sequencing)
[Enrichment] Agent must capture goals mentioned by user via create_goal tool
[Enrichment] Agent must update profile with occupation, work hours, preferences when shared
[Enrichment] Agent should cover all 5 areas within the turn budget (school, work, health, personal, routine)
[Conversation] Schedule queries ("what do I have today") must call list_events or search_events, not create anything
[Conversation] Event creation must use the correct aspect for the activity type
[Conversation] Event modification must search for the existing event first, then update it -- not create a new one
[Conversation] Event deletion must confirm the right event before deleting (search first)
[Conversation] Agent must NOT create duplicate events when one already exists at that time
[Conversation] Agent must be aware of existing schedule when suggesting times ("do I have time for X")
[Conversation] Task creation should use create_task, not create_event for non-time-bound items
[Conversation] Goal check-ins should call list_goals or check_in_goal, not create new goals
[Conversation] Responses must be concise and natural -- no markdown headers, no numbered lists unless asked
[Conversation] Agent must not call unnecessary tools -- a simple greeting needs zero tool calls
[Conversation] Agent must handle ambiguous time references correctly ("tomorrow", "next Monday", "this afternoon")
[Conversation] When asked about free time, agent must call find_free_time and give specific windows
[Scheduler] Every suggestion must reference a specific goal, task, or aspect -- no generic filler
[Scheduler] Suggestion descriptions must explain WHY (reference the goal/task it advances)
[Scheduler] High-energy tasks (deep work, studying) must be placed in mornings/early afternoon
[Scheduler] Low-energy tasks (review, planning) must be placed in evenings
[Scheduler] No overlapping slots with existing events
[Scheduler] Minimum 60-minute duration for all slots
[Scheduler] Maximum 3-4 slots per day, spread across 7 days
[Scheduler] Multiple life areas must be represented (not all one aspect)
[Scheduler] Slots must have 30+ minute gaps between them and existing events
[Anti-pattern] DUPLICATE CREATION: Creating a second "CS 161 Lecture" when one already exists at MWF 10:30am
[Anti-pattern] TOOL NARRATION: "I will now create an aspect for your class" instead of just doing it
[Anti-pattern] GENERIC SUGGESTIONS: "Watch a TED talk" or "Go for a walk" with no goal connection
[Anti-pattern] OVER-TOOLING: Calling 5 tools when 1 would suffice
[Anti-pattern] UNDER-TOOLING: Responding to "add a meeting" with text instead of calling create_event
[Anti-pattern] WRONG TOOL: Using create_event for a task, or create_task for a scheduled meeting
[Anti-pattern] MARKDOWN IN CHAT: Using ## headers, **bold**, - bullet points in conversational responses
[Anti-pattern] IGNORING CONTEXT: Suggesting a meeting at 2pm when user has class then
[Anti-pattern] ASPECT ORPHANS: Creating events without linking to an aspect
[Anti-pattern] STALE RESPONSES: Not reflecting the current state of the calendar in answers
